methods.description: Process for collecting and preserving the specimen of the new species for morphological and molecular analyses.

steps.description: The single specimen of the new species, collected by a Malaise trap, was preserved in 85% ethanol and stored in the dark at 4 °C before morphological and molecular analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and preserve specimen in ethanol at 4°C

methods.description: Extraction of genomic DNA from the specimen for further molecular analysis.

steps.description: Genomic DNA was extracted from the thorax and head using a Qiagen DNA Blood and Tissue Kit at Nankai University, Tianjin, China, following the standard protocol except for the final elution volume of 100 µl.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA using Qiagen kit with modified elution volume
inputs: Qiagen DNA Blood and Tissue Kit

methods.description: Preparation of the specimen's exoskeleton and appendages for morphological analysis.

steps.description: After DNA extraction, the exoskeleton of each specimen was mounted in Euparal on a microscope slide together with the corresponding wings, legs, antennae and abdomen, following the procedures outlined by Saether (1969).
steps.searchable_summary: mount exoskeleton and appendages on slide with Euparal

methods.description: Capture digital photographs of slide-mounted specimens for documentation and analysis.

steps.description: Digital photographs of slide-mounted specimens were taken with a 300-dpi resolution using a Nikon Digital Sight DS-Fil camera mounted on Nikon Eclipse 80i compound microscope using the software NIS-Elements F v.4.60.00.
steps.searchable_summary: capture digital images of specimens using Nikon camera and software

methods.description: Amplification of the mitochondrial COI barcode region using PCR for genetic analysis.

steps.description: The universal primers LCO1490 and HCO2198 were used to amplify the standard 658-bp mitochondrial COI barcode region. PCR amplifications followed Song et al. (2018) and were conducted in a 25 µl volume including 12.5 µl 2× Es Taq MasterMix, 0.625 µl of each primer, 2 µl of template DNA and 9.25 µl of deionized H2O.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification of COI barcode region

methods.description: Processing and analysis of DNA sequences for alignment and phylogenetic tree construction.

steps.description: Raw sequences were assembled and edited in Geneious Prime 2020.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble and edit DNA sequences in Geneious Prime

steps.description: Alignment of the sequences was carried out using the MUSCLE algorithm on amino acids in MEGA 6.
steps.searchable_summary: align DNA sequences using MUSCLE in MEGA 6

steps.description: The pairwise distances using the Kimura 2-Parameter (K2P) substitution model of six species within the subgenus Cerobregma were calculated in MEGA.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pairwise distances using K2P model in MEGA

steps.description: The neighbor-joining tree was constructed using the K2P substitution model, 1000 bootstrap replicates and the 'pairwise deletion' option for missing data in MEGA.
steps.searchable_summary: construct neighbor-joining tree with K2P model in MEGA

methods.description: Submission of novel sequences and metadata to public databases for accessibility and future reference.

steps.description: Novel sequences, trace-files, and metadata of the new species were uploaded to the Barcode of Life Data Systems (BOLD).
steps.searchable_summary: upload sequences and metadata to BOLD
